title: Pfizer's Sigvotatug Vedotin Misses Primary Survival Goal in Late-Stage Lung Cancer Trial

contentHtml:
Bengaluru: Pfizer ‚Äãsaid its drug for ‚Äãa type of lung cancer missed the main goal of improving survival ‚Äåin ‚ÅÝpatients ‚ÅÝcompared to a generic ‚Äãdrug in a late-stage trial.

The drug was ‚Äãbeing tested in locally advanced, unresectable or metastatic non-squamous non-small ‚Äãcell lung ‚ÅÝcancer (NSCLC) who had ‚Äåreceived one or ‚Äãmore ‚Äãlines of prior ‚ÅÝtherapy versus docetaxel.

However, separately in patients who received only one prior line of systemic therapy, the company said the drug sigvotatug vedotin showed a stronger trend in ‚Äåimproving survival and survival without progression of the disease versus docetaxel.

Pfizer ‚ÅÝsaid this reinforces its confidence in the potential of the sigvotatug ‚Äãvedotin program, including an ongoing late-stage trial in combination with Merck's Keytruda.

Source : Reuters

Sheeba Farhat Joined Medical Dialogues in 2018 to report on the latest Education news. A Graduate of the University of Delhi, she specializes in covering stories related to Medical Education updates. For inquiries or further information, you can reach her at editorial@medicaldialogues.in.
